title: ranunculus seguieri subsp. cantabricus
intro: Ranunculus seguieri subsp. cantabricus is a lesser-known, perennial plant species belonging to the buttercup family, native to the Iberian Peninsula. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus seguieri subsp. cantabricus are attributed to its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, improve circulation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used as a tea or infused in oil to create a topical remedy for skin issues and wounds.
